evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses the correct new endpoint places.googleapis.com/v1/places:autocomplete with X-Goog-Api-Key and X-Goog-FieldMask headers. Internally consistent; method names and headers align with Places API (New) conventions.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse cleanly. Backend imports are complete; frontend uses crypto.randomUUID (browser-native). Minor issue: no .env.example block per se (only a .env example), and frontend setup uses create-react-app which is now legacy but still runs. No sandbox-infeasible patterns.
evidence.completeness: (a) API key acquisition with menu path ✓; (b) install commands in bash ✓; (c) env var GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY shown in env block ✓; (d) FastAPI backend code ✓; (e) React component ✓; (f) test command (uvicorn + manual /docs test) ✓ though test is partly manual. No duplicate samples; env vars consistent.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Only one link to Google Cloud Console; no link to canonical developers.google.com/maps documentation placed near code. No fabricated URLs.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; long-tail handling applied. Playbook is internally consistent and uses the new v1 endpoint correctly, scoring 4 on correctness. Citation weak — only console.cloud.google.com referenced, no docs URL.
